1: A shadow: "The LORD reigns; let the peoples tremble! He sits enthroned upon the cherubim; let the earth quake!" Hebrews 1:3 — Christ "sat down at the right hand of the Majesty on high." Rev 4-5 — the throne scene where the Lamb receives the scroll of universal sovereignty. Philippians 2:10 — "at the name of Jesus every knee should bow." The trembling of the peoples before the enthroned LORD is the response to Christ's universal lordship.
2: A shadow: "The LORD is great in Zion; he is exalted over all the peoples." Ephesians 1:20-22 — God "seated him at his right hand in the heavenly places, far above all rule and authority... and put all things under his feet." Hebrews 12:22 — the heavenly Zion where Christ is great. The greatness of the LORD in Zion is the greatness of Christ in the heavenly Jerusalem.
3: A shadow: "Let them praise your great and awesome name! Holy is he!" Philippians 2:9-10 — "the name that is above every name, that at the name of Jesus every knee should bow." Revelation 4:8 — "Holy, holy, holy, is the Lord God Almighty, who was and is and is to come!" The praise of the great and awesome name is fulfilled in the worship of the one whose name is above all names.
4: A shadow: "The King in his might loves justice. You have established equity; you have executed justice and righteousness in Jacob." Isaiah 9:7 — "of the increase of his government and of peace there will be no end, on the throne of David and over his kingdom, to establish it and to uphold it with justice and with righteousness from this time forth and forevermore." Christ is the just king who establishes righteousness in his kingdom (Hebrews 1:8-9).
5: A shadow: "Exalt the LORD our God; worship at his footstool! Holy is he!" Hebrews 1:6 — "Let all God's angels worship him!" Revelation 5:14 — "the four living creatures said 'Amen!' and the elders fell down and worshiped." The call to exalt and worship before the LORD's footstool is the call fulfilled in the eternal worship before the throne of the Lamb.
6: A type: "Moses and Aaron were among his priests, Samuel also was among those who called upon his name." Moses (Deuteronomy 18:15 — "the LORD your God will raise up for you a prophet like me"), Aaron (the high-priestly type fulfilled in Christ, Heb 7-10), Samuel (the prophet-intercessor; 1 Samuel 12:23 — "far be it from me to sin against the LORD by ceasing to pray for you"). All three are types of Christ as prophet, priest, and intercessor.
7: A type: "In the pillar of the cloud he spoke to them." The pillar of cloud = the Shekinah, the divine presence among Israel. John 1:14 — "the Word became flesh and eskēnōsen (tabernacled) among us, and we have seen his glory." The cloud presence in the wilderness is the type of which the incarnation is the antitype — God dwelling among his people in the fullness of his glory in the person of his Son.
8: A revelation of God: "you were a forgiving God to them, but an avenger of their wrongdoings." The dual character of God — forgiveness and justice held together. Romans 3:25-26 — the cross demonstrates that God is "just and the justifier of the one who has faith in Jesus." The forgiveness of God does not override his justice; the cross is where both are simultaneously satisfied. Christ bears the vengeance so that the forgiveness can be real.
9: A shadow: "Worship at his holy mountain; for the LORD our God is holy!" Hebrews 12:22 — "you have come to Mount Zion and to the city of the living God, the heavenly Jerusalem." Revelation 14:1 — "I looked, and behold, on Mount Zion stood the Lamb, and with him 144,000 who had his name and his Father's name written on their foreheads." The holy mountain of God is the mountain where the Lamb stands.
